item.title: With US spy laws set to expire, lawmakers are split over protecting Americans from warrantless surveillance
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Some lawmakers are calling for widespread reforms following years of surveillance scandals and abuses across successive U.S. administrations. But even if the spy law known as Section 702 expires in April, the government's spy powers will not automatically lapse.

item.title: Google’s AI Mode can now help you find products in stock nearby
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Although you can already track hotel prices at the city level, the new update lets you do so for a specific hotel that you're interested in.

item.title: Bluesky confirms DDoS attack is cause of continued app outages
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Bluesky has been experiencing ongoing service disruptions since just before 3 a.m. ET. on April 15.

item.title: Netflix plans to add a vertical video feed, use AI for recommendations
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Netflix is going to launch a TikTok-like vertical video feed within its apps this month, and plans to use AI broadly for content creation and recommendations.

item.title: SaySo is a new short-form video app that aims to restore users’ trust in news
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Users are fed up with misinformation and AI slop cluttering their feeds. SaySo is a new short-form video app that delivers news from vetted creators and journalists.

item.title: Loop raises $95M to build supply chain AI that predicts disruptions
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The San Francisco startup closed a Series C funding round led by Antonio Gracias' firm Valor, which is a major backer of xAI.

item.title: New leaders, new fund: Sequoia has raised $7B to expand its AI bets
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The fundraise is the first major capital raise under Sequoia's new leadership, with Alfred Lin and Pat Grady now serving as co-stewards of the 54-year-old firm.

item.title: Factory hits $1.5B valuation to build AI coding for enterprises
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The three-year-old startup raised $150 million led by Khosla Ventures.

item.title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The studio's first project will be about Moses and star Academy Award-winner Ben Kingsley, to be released this spring on Prime Video.

item.title: Netflix co-founder and chair Reed Hastings to leave board
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Hastings helped to transform the video rental industry — first with physical, then digital, delivery.

item.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to raise its third funding round since launching just seven months ago.

item.title: Physical Intelligence, a hot robotics startup, says its new robot brain can figure out tasks it was never taught
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The new model, called π0.7, represents what the company describes as an early but meaningful step toward the long-sought goal of a general-purpose robot brain.

item.title: From the Startup Battlefield stage to the International Space Station: geCKo Materials built a sticky product
item.dc:creator: Maggie Nye, Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: This week on Build Mode, Isabelle Johannessen speaks with Capella Kerst, founder and CEO of geCKo Materials.

item.title: Slash, a Ramp competitor founded by teenagers, raises $100M at $1.4B valuation
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Five years later the founders, now 24 years old, say their startup has achieved $300 million in annualized revenue.

item.title: OpenAI takes aim at Anthropic with beefed-up Codex that gives it more power over your desktop
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: OpenAI's agentic coding tool has gotten a major makeover, with a variety of new powers and abilities.

item.title: European police email 75,000 people asking them to stop DDoS attacks
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Europol coordinated an operation against for-hire distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) services, including the arrest of four people and the takedown of 53 domains.

item.title: Anthropic CPO leaves Figma’s board after reports he will offer a competing product
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Krieger's departure and any forthcoming design tools will be another data point for investors who fear the SaaSpocalypse — that the largest AI labs will come to dominate software businesses, a thesis that has rocked public markets at times this year.

item.title: Everything we like is a psyop
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: If culture is shaped by TikTok's algorithmic feed, then it's time for us to decide where we draw the line between necessary marketing and inauthentic growth hacking.

item.title: Google now lets you explore the web side-by-side with AI Mode
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Now, when you're using AI Mode on Chrome desktop, clicking a link will open the web page side-by-side with AI Mode.

item.title: Two Americans sentenced for helping North Korea steal $5 million in fake IT worker scheme
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced that two Americans were sentenced to years in prison for helping the North Korean government place fake IT workers in U.S. companies.
